title: tapinanthus luteiflorus
intro: Tapinanthus luteiflorus, also known as yellow-flowered mistletoe, is a parasitic plant species that grows on various host trees, obtaining its nutrients by tapping into the host's vascular system. Traditionally, the plant is used in folk medicine in Africa, where it is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is said to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and alleviating pain. In herbal preparations, Tapinanthus luteiflorus is often used in infusions, decoctions, or dried and used in teas, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
